frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Haha, this meme is spot on! I mean, it's not even about judging, but it's like, how much 'real' is even left under all that? Imagine committing to someone and then realizing you've never actually seen their real face. Thoda unfair lagta hai yaar.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment implies that women who wear makeup are being deceptive or hiding their true selves, framing it as unfair to men who might commit to them. It subtly polices women's appearance by questioning their 'realness' and asserting a man's 'right' to know a woman's natural look before a serious relationship, encoding surface manifestations of deception and control over appearance.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 23. Single. A college student in Bengaluru. You are in a WhatsApp group with seven male college friends. Someone has shared a meme that says women spend two hours putting on makeup and then get angry when men call them fake. You are reacting to the meme and adding your own comment about women who wear heavy makeup and what they are hiding and whether men have a right to know what a woman actually looks like before committing to a relationship.
